evidences.content: | Market Digest: PLD, IFF, NFLX, NJR, USB, UAL | Market Digest: PLD, IFF, NFLX, NJR, USB, UAL Like other recent rebounds from quick declines, Wednesday's first attempted upside reversal bumped its head against key shorter-term moving averages and paused. Some of these breaks higher have been impressive, while others have not. It's almost like machines (algorithms) are running the intraday movements in the major indices. In fact, there is consistent support and resistance on the charts that seems to act as a magnet for prices. When all was said and done on Wednesday, the S&P 500 (SPX) gained back 1.2% and closed back above its 50-day. But the index ran out of gas at the 5-, 13-, and 21-day exponential moving averages as well as at chart resistance from the breakdown level at intraday lows [...] chart resistance from the breakdown level at intraday lows on January 14. The SPX didn't reach the lower trendline from the busted and bearish-ending diagonal. All these clusters of resistance lie in the 6,890 to 6,940 region. [...] Shortly after the opening bell, the stock surged to just under $1,000 a share as analysts rushed to increase their respective price targets. Pivotal Research upped its target from $1,000 a share to $1,250 — the highest on the Street. The streaming giant reported a whopping 18.9 million users in the fourth quarter, while revenue and earnings also handily beat expectations. It was the biggest quarterly subscriber gain in the company's history.
